summary: ANDROID 🟥 DFU flow can proceed on Software Update page even though device was disconnected
scenario: DFU flow proceeds on Software Update page after device disconnection instead of blocking with "Update currently not available" error
description_clean: DFU flow can proceed on the Software Update page even though the device was disconnected. After entering the DFU flow and navigating to the Software Update page, if the user disconnects the headset and tries to continue, they are navigated to the Three Easy Steps page instead of being blocked with an error message: "Update currently not available, please try back later."
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Ensure a Poly Bluetooth headset is connected.
Navigate to Home → [Device Name] → Software Update.
Tap 'Update' or enter the DFU (Device Firmware Update) flow.
On the Software Update page, disconnect the headset (e.g., turn off Bluetooth on the phone or power off the headset).
Attempt to continue with the update process (e.g., tap 'Continue' or follow on-screen prompts).
expected: The user should be blocked with an error message: "Update currently not available, please try back later."
actual: The user is navigated to the Three Easy Steps page instead of being blocked with the error message.
